question.text: Which of the following is NOT a product category that new cadre distributors are permitted to sell?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** New cadre distributors can sell only five categories: (1) Liquid/Money Market, (2) Index, (3) Diversified Equity, (4) Retirement Benefit, and (5) Fixed Maturity Plans. Sectoral/thematic funds are concentrated in one sector and carry higher risk — they are NOT on the permitted list. **The Trap:** Diversified equity is permitted, so people may assume all equity funds are permitted. But sectoral funds are a separate, higher-risk category. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under distributor eligibility. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If a client asks you to help them invest in a sectoral fund, you must politely explain that you are not authorized to sell it. Refer them to a regular MF distributor with Series V-A certification.
